meta_title: House Clearance Eastbourne | Furniture & Probate | Mark Ratcliffe
meta_description: Compassionate, full-service house clearance in Eastbourne. Probate, downsizing and furniture removal. Licensed waste carriers. Free quote.
h1: House Clearance in Eastbourne — Done with Care
intro_paragraph: House clearance is often a difficult job done at a difficult time. Mark Ratcliffe Moving's clearance service in Eastbourne is built on the same family values as our removal business: we treat your home and the possessions inside it with respect, we sort carefully rather than skip-and-bin, and we work patiently with families, executors and solicitors. We are an Environment Agency upper-tier registered waste carrier, so everything we remove is handled legally and traceably.

sections.body_html: House clearance is often a difficult job done at a difficult time. Mark Ratcliffe Moving's clearance service in Eastbourne is built on the same family values as our removal business: we treat your home and the possessions inside it with respect, we sort carefully rather than skip-and-bin, and we work patiently with families, executors and solicitors. We are an Environment Agency upper-tier registered waste carrier, so everything we remove is handled legally and traceably.

sections.heading: When you need our clearance service
sections.body_html:
When you need our clearance service

  • Probate clearance — after a bereavement, to ready a property for sale or transfer
  • Downsizing — moving into a smaller home and needing to thin out years of accumulated possessions
  • End of tenancy — landlords needing to clear and reset a rental property between tenants
  • Property sale preparation — clearing a home before it goes on the market
  • Garage, loft and outbuilding clearance — partial clearance, not the whole house
  • Hoarder property — sensitive, judgement-free clearance with longer time allowance

sections.heading: How our house clearance works
sections.body_html:
How our house clearance works

Walk-through and fixed quote

We start with a free walk-through of the property — taking notes on volume, access, special items and any complications. You then receive a fixed-price written quote with no obligation. For probate clearances we can also identify items of resale value and recommend an auction route where appropriate.

Sort, retain, donate, recycle, dispose

On clearance day, our team works through the property room by room. Items earmarked for family are bagged separately and either delivered or stored. Items in good condition go to our charity partner. Recyclable materials go to the right recycling stream. Items with resale value can be set aside for auction. Only what genuinely cannot be reused is taken to licensed waste — and we provide a transfer note for it.

Licensed waste carrier

We are registered with the Environment Agency as an upper-tier waste carrier (registration available on request). Many one-person clearance operations are not — and using an unlicensed carrier can leave the property owner liable if waste is fly-tipped. Always ask for a registration number before booking any clearance company.

Same-day or scheduled clearance

For smaller jobs we can sometimes clear within a day or two. Larger probate clearances usually take two to four days depending on size and the time needed to sort sensitively.

sections.heading: Probate clearance with sensitivity
sections.body_html:
Probate clearance with sensitivity

Probate is the most common reason families call us. We understand that a clearance after a bereavement is not just an administrative task — it is a moment of letting go. Our team works at the family's pace, with patience and care. We can:

  • Work alongside executors and probate solicitors
  • Identify items of possible value for a probate valuation
  • Bag and store family-retained items at our Lower Dicker storage for collection
  • Arrange auction or charity routes for the rest
  • Issue waste transfer notes and a written completion record

Many of our probate clients come to us through Sussex solicitors we have worked with for decades.

sections.heading: What we donate versus dispose
sections.body_html:
What we donate versus dispose

Our default is to maximise donation and recycling and minimise landfill. Typical proportions on a domestic clearance run roughly 30% donate, 40% recycle, 20% reuse-or-auction, 10% landfill — though it varies considerably by property. We work with established Sussex charity partners (Hospice and other registered charities) and we keep the donation paperwork for executors who need it for accounts.

sections.heading: Pricing
sections.body_html:
Pricing

House clearance pricing is based on volume (number of skip-equivalents), access at the property, special items (white goods, paints, chemicals, hazardous waste) and the level of sort required. As a guide, a 2-bedroom flat clearance typically runs £450–£800; a 3-bedroom semi £700–£1,400; a larger 4 or 5-bedroom probate clearance £1,200–£2,500. All quotes are itemised and fixed — no surprises on the day.

sections.heading: The compassionate approach to probate clearance
sections.body_html:
The compassionate approach to probate clearance

Probate clearance is rarely just a logistics job — it is the closing chapter on someone's life. Our crews are trained to handle this work with quiet respect. We work at the family's pace, never rush decisions, and we will pause if items unexpectedly turn out to have sentimental value that nobody anticipated. If a family member wants to come back after the first day for a second look, we will hold items aside without complaint. Many of our probate clients are recommended by Sussex solicitors who know we do this properly.

sections.heading: Frequently Asked Questions
sections.body_html:
Frequently Asked Questions

Are you licensed to remove waste?

Yes. We are registered as an upper-tier waste carrier with the Environment Agency. You can ask for a copy of our registration certificate before booking.

Can you handle probate house clearance?

Yes — sensitively. We work directly with executors and solicitors, agree what is to be retained for family, what is to be sold or donated, and what is to be disposed of. We can also arrange a probate valuation.

What happens to items I do not want?

We sort: items that can be donated go to our charity partner, items that can be recycled go to the appropriate recycling stream, items with resale value can be auctioned, and only what remains goes to licensed waste.

Do you clear hoarder properties?

Yes — we have experience of hoarder clearances and we approach them sensitively, without judgement. Allow longer for the sort and clearance. Hazardous biological waste, if present, is handled by a specialist partner.

raw_body:
Home › Services › House Clearance
Probate · Downsizing · Licensed waste carrier

House Clearance in Eastbourne — Done with Care

Get a Free Quote Call 01323 848 008

Mark Ratcliffe Moving's clearance service in Eastbourne is built on the same family values as our removal business: we treat your home and the possessions inside it with respect, we sort carefully rather than skip-and-bin, and we work patiently with families, executors and solicitors. We are an Environment Agency upper-tier registered waste carrier, so everything we remove is handled legally and traceably.
